name: Xenobot Kinematics Simulator
description: Simulates the emergent two-dimensional locomotion of a Xenobot — a living, programmable organism assembled from Xenopus laevis embryonic cells — by modelling the collective force contributions of passive (epidermis-derived) and contractile motor (cardiac-muscle-derived) cells. Given a spatial layout of cells with type labels, the simulator integrates a simplified Newtonian trajectory over discrete time-steps and returns the bot's predicted displacement path as a base64-encoded PNG visualisation alongside quantitative kinematic metrics. Intended for undergraduate exploration of synthetic organismal design, this module supports rapid in-silico prototyping of cell-arrangement hypotheses before wet-lab construction.

inputs.name: cells
inputs.description: An ordered list of cell descriptors defining the xenobot body. Each object must contain three fields: 'x' (float, x-coordinate in the body frame, arbitrary units), 'y' (float, y-coordinate in the body frame, arbitrary units), and 'type' (string, one of 'passive', 'motor_x', or 'motor_y'). Passive cells represent structural epidermis-derived tissue; motor_x cells represent cardiac-muscle-derived cells that exert contractile force along the positive x-axis; motor_y cells exert force along the positive y-axis.

outputs.description: A result dictionary with four keys. 'content' is a list containing an 'image/png' item (base64-encoded PNG visualising the bot body layout and simulated locomotion path) and a 'text/plain' item (human-readable summary of kinematic statistics). 'net_displacement' is a float giving the Euclidean distance (arbitrary units) from start to end position. 'trajectory' is a list of [x, y] waypoints sampled at each integration step. 'cell_summary' is a dict with integer counts for each cell type ('passive', 'motor_x', 'motor_y').

content.data: net_displacement=0.4821 au; steps=50; dt=0.1; passive=2; motor_x=1; motor_y=0
net_displacement=0.6103 au; steps=50; dt=0.1; passive=1; motor_x=2; motor_y=1
